items.question: any vs unknown — what's the difference?
items.answer: Both accept any value, but any disables type checking entirely (you can use it freely and unsafely), whereas unknown forces you to narrow/validate it before use. Prefer unknown for untrusted data.

items.question: interface vs type — when do you use each?
items.answer: Use interface for object shapes you may extend or merge; use type for unions, intersections, tuples and aliases of primitives. For plain object shapes either works, but interface is the common convention.

items.question: What breaks if you rely on types to validate an LLM's JSON response?
items.answer: Nothing at compile time — but at runtime the data can be any shape, because types are erased. Without runtime validation, malformed responses slip through and crash later.
